evidence: In April 2024 we acquired Southwest Data Products, Inc. to expand and enhance NRG's ability to serve the rapidly growing data center market with steel racking and other products.

NRG produces and installs standard and custom designed steel racking systems for warehouse applications, including rack supported buildings and warehouses designed to leverage emerging automatic storage and retrieval (ASRS) technology.
NRG's added capabilities now include airflow containment structures, cabinet/enclosures, caging and other products for both new data centers and data center retrofits.
growing demand from data center construction

The steel products segment had increased volumes in 2025 compared to 2024, reflecting stabilized demand in the warehouse construction market in 2025 after a pull back in demand in 2024, and growing demand from data center construction.

capex_mentions.quote: acquired Southwest Data Products, Inc. ... for $115 million
capex_mentions.context: April 2024 acquisition of data center infrastructure manufacturer to expand NRG's data center product capabilities

limitations: Nucor is a steel manufacturer, not an AI company; AI investment signals are indirect — the company supplies steel products and racking to data centers rather than investing in AI itself.
The $115M SWDP acquisition is data-center-infrastructure-related but not AI-attributable in the strict sense; it is steel racking and enclosures for data centers, not AI compute or AI R&D.
ASRS (automatic storage and retrieval) technology reference is automation-adjacent but not explicitly AI.
Risk-factor section excerpt contains no AI-specific risk language despite discussing data center demand, hence scored 1.
No quantified AI-specific capex, R&D, or headcount figures appear in the excerpts.
Excerpts are limited; broader filing may contain more AI-relevant disclosures not captured here.
